old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-oczt","title":"DodagState.process_dio allows self-selection as parent","description":"File: python/src/lichen/rpl/dodag.py:116-153

The process_dio() method doesn't check if the neighbor_id matches the node's own address, potentially allowing a node to select itself as a parent.

```python
def process_dio(
    self, dio: DIO, neighbor_id: IPv6Address | str, link_etx: float = 1.0
) -> None:
    ...
    self.parents[neighbor_id] = ParentCandidate(neighbor_id, dio.rank, link_etx)
    self.select_parent()
```

If a malicious node spoofs a DIO appearing to come from the receiving node's own address, or if there's a bug in the caller, the node could add itself to its parent set. While select_parent() uses MRHOF to pick the best path, a self-loop would create:
1. Infinite rank loop (self.rank = self.rank + increase)
2. Routing loop for upward traffic

RFC 6550 Section 8.2.2.5 says nodes MUST NOT select themselves as a parent.

Fix:
```python
if neighbor_id == self.node_address:  # Need to add node_address to DodagState
    return  # Ignore DIOs from self
```

Note: DodagState doesn't currently store the node's own address, so the fix requires adding this field.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:25:59.714439Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:25:59.714478Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-oczt","title":"DodagState.process_dio allows self-selection as parent","description":"File: python/src/lichen/rpl/dodag.py:116-153

The process_dio() method doesn't check if the neighbor_id matches the node's own address, potentially allowing a node to select itself as a parent.

```python
def process_dio(
    self, dio: DIO, neighbor_id: IPv6Address | str, link_etx: float = 1.0
) -> None:
    ...
    self.parents[neighbor_id] = ParentCandidate(neighbor_id, dio.rank, link_etx)
    self.select_parent()
```

If a malicious node spoofs a DIO appearing to come from the receiving node's own address, or if there's a bug in the caller, the node could add itself to its parent set. While select_parent() uses MRHOF to pick the best path, a self-loop would create:
1. Infinite rank loop (self.rank = self.rank + increase)
2. Routing loop for upward traffic

RFC 6550 Section 8.2.2.5 says nodes MUST NOT select themselves as a parent.

Fix:
```python
if neighbor_id == self.node_address:  # Need to add node_address to DodagState
    return  # Ignore DIOs from self
```

Note: DodagState doesn't currently store the node's own address, so the fix requires adding this field.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:25:59.714439Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T20:19:48.974836872Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T20:19:48.974836872Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T20:24:48.974836872Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T20:19:48.974836872Z"}

new_value: Cleaned self-parent check and removed comments per guidelines. Already had the validation. Commit 3e189903c
